memory_candidates.content: settings.local.json accumulates stale bash permission entries from agent sessions. These include mv commands for completed file reorganizations, specs path references for archived tasks, and shell loop construct fragments. Periodic cleanup is needed. The python3:* wildcard subsumes specific python3 validation commands, and Read(//path/**) wildcards subsume variable-based Read entries.

memory_candidates.content: When using ((VAR++)) in bash with set -e, the expression returns exit code 1 when VAR is 0 (because ((0)) is falsy), causing the script to abort. Use VAR=$((VAR + 1)) instead to avoid this interaction.

memory_candidates.content: Virtual extension pattern: extensions with virtual: true in manifest skip file copy on load and file removal on unload. Use this for describing files that already exist in-place (like core system files) without physical migration.

Allow-list sync pattern: when a core manifest exists, scan_all_artifacts() post-filters results against the core provides allow-list instead of using the blocklist. Falls back to blocklist if no core manifest.

memory_candidates.content: The extension loader's remove_orphaned_index_entries() function only removes entries with paths starting with 'project/' -- all other entries (architecture/, formats/, patterns/, etc.) are unconditionally preserved. This means core agent system context entries survive orphan cleanup regardless of which extension loaded them.

active_projects.completion_summary: Converted core extension index entry loading from 12-line special-case code block in init.lua to the standard merge_targets.index mechanism. Relocated index-entries.json to extension root, updated manifest, removed special-case code, and updated 5 documentation files.
active_projects.claudemd_suggestions: No CLAUDE.md changes needed -- the core extension merge_targets now handle index entry loading automatically through the standard pipeline.
